title: Dryopteris chrysoloba
intro: Dryopteris chrysoloba, also known as the golden shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris and is native to the mountainous regions of Japan and Korea. The golden shield fern is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, making it beneficial for reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage in the body. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The golden shield fern is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Dryopteris chrysoloba is a species of fern within the genus Dryopteris, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Dryopteris is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. This classification places Dryopteris chrysoloba within the broader plant kingdom, Eukarya. The family Dryopteridaceae is further categorized under the class Polypodiopsida, which comprises all modern ferns. This comprehensive classification helps understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Dryopteris chrysoloba within the plant kingdom.
